summary.intent: Understand why vendor/sqlx-sqlite was added to the branch and determine if it could be replaced with a cleaner dependency management approach.
summary.outcome: Replaced the vendored sqlx-sqlite directory (~10.5K lines) with a Cargo git patch pointing to a forked moltis-org/sqlx repo containing the one-line libsqlite3-sys version bound fix, then committed and pushed the change.

repo: The moltis project uses a feat/matrix-channel branch that adds Matrix SDK support via matrix-sdk-sqlite, which transitively pulls in rusqlite 0.37 → libsqlite3-sys 0.35.0
sqlx-sqlite 0.8.6 pins libsqlite3-sys = 0.30.1 exactly, causing a conflict with the Matrix SDK dependency tree
The vendor patch only modified Cargo.toml (widening libsqlite3-sys from = 0.30.1 to >= 0.30.1, < 0.36.0); all Rust source files were identical to upstream
When patching sqlx-sqlite via git dep, sqlx-core must also be patched from the same source to avoid type mismatches with sqlx-macros-core from the registry
The project lives under moltis-org GitHub org; the sqlx fork was created there as moltis-org/sqlx

code.finding: Final [patch.crates-io] section references both sqlx-sqlite and sqlx-core from moltis-org/sqlx fork at rev 33747ad2 on branch moltis/widen-libsqlite3-sys
Only change from upstream: libsqlite3-sys version requirement widened from '= 0.30.1' to '>= 0.30.1, < 0.36.0'

workflow: Cargo git patches require patching all crates from the same source when there are intra-workspace type dependencies — patching only one crate causes type mismatch errors
Forking upstream and using git+rev in [patch.crates-io] is cleaner than vendoring when the change is minimal (one line) — avoids committing thousands of lines of source
Cargo fetches git dependencies automatically so no CI submodule checkout steps are needed, unlike git submodules
To compare a vendored crate against upstream, find it in ~/.cargo/registry/src/ for diffing

friction: Initial git patch of only sqlx-sqlite caused build failure because sqlx-macros-core (from registry) saw incompatible sqlx-core types — required also patching sqlx-core from the fork
The fork pulled main branch by default; had to explicitly create a branch from the v0.8.6 tag to get the correct base commit
No stable sqlx release beyond 0.8.6 exists (only 0.9.0-alpha.1), so the workaround cannot be eliminated by a simple version bump
open_items: Remove the sqlx fork patches (sqlx-sqlite and sqlx-core in [patch.crates-io]) once sqlx >= 0.9.0 ships stable with a relaxed libsqlite3-sys version bound
Monitor sqlx 0.9.0 release — it should natively support newer libsqlite3-sys versions, making the moltis-org/sqlx fork obsolete
The moltis-org/sqlx fork branch moltis/widen-libsqlite3-sys should be deleted once the upstream fix lands
